title: drosera nitidula var. allantostigma
intro:
Drosera nitidula var. allantostigma is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Sundew plant (Drosera genus), characterized by its unique, modified leaves that form a tubular structure, trapping insects for nutrient acquisition.

Medicinally, Drosera nitidula var. allantostigma is valued for its expectorant properties, which help to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma by increasing mucus production and facilitating its expulsion from the lungs.

The health benefits of Drosera nitidula var. allantostigma include its ability to alleviate coughs, reduce inflammation, and act as an antispasmodic to calm respiratory spasms. 

Herbal preparations of Drosera nitidula var. allantostigma often involve drying the leaves and roots to create teas, tinctures, or infusions that can be used to treat respiratory issues, although its uses are largely anecdotal and require further research for validation.
